description: Apollo 9 Flight Journal: Day 1: Orbit 3 - Separation & Ejection

tokens: Could you give us P00 and Accept, please?
We have a state vector for you, and I have a nav check when you are ready to copy.
And we would also like to have your opinion on do you think you will have any problems continuing on the timeline through ejection with this situation.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: You have got P00 and Accept.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: The computer is yours, and that quad A temp has dropped about 8° now coming across the States, and we're keeping an eye on it.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We've got you for about another minute.
We'll see you over Ascension, around 28.
We would like to have you to go Block on your command module telemetry, and you don't have to slip a nav check.
We've checked your vector, and it's good.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: You're way down in the mud.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, tunnel hatch removal was the next.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hatch pressure equalization valve, open counterclockwise.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Po - Pump handle, unstow, Pull to STOP.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Pump handle, unstow; pull to stop; and it says "parentheses A." Push - I mean set to U, and push to stop.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Dave, you know, you might check some circuit breakers over there and see if anything else might have popped.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, we're ready to go; we're in U.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, here's that - here's that washer, Dave.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Verify gearbox disconnect socket, V - U.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, let's move this down, huh?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, that should go all the way down.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, it's just not easy to handle up here, Dave, compared to the other way.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I think what we have to be careful of is that we don't get it moving so fast that it cuts the hose or something.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: No, you're blocked by the hose (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let me, I'll just undo that (garbled) --
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I'll get it; just a minute.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You want me to hold this?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think you're alright now, Jim, with the hatch, if you just keep coming.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We need to slide it up - face up toward Rusty to get it in.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: The LMP now has the hatch.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Rusty, would you hold this?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Do you have your hand under it, Dave?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Latch handle; pull to verify hook engaged, 12 latches.
And, you know, as you go around, Jim, check the red pit and check the bungee.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You ought to - On up - You can check each latch, you know, all three of those things, and we'll have it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I am; I just want to get it someplace.
The bungee is straight up and down.
Latch 2, the handle's engaged, and red button is there.
And it looks like the bungee - Gee, it's a weird view up here.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, you're checking that the bungees are parallel, right?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, 4 looks good; 5 looks good; 6 looks good.
6 looks good; 7 looks good - (garbled) 9 looks good.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Let me un - let me unscrew myself here.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, 10 looks good; 11, 12, they all look good.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, GN bleed button, red, press for 10 seconds.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It's at the base of the probe, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Right, the little one here; it says --
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: You've taken the pressure off your --
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That's supposed to be 15 seconds underway.
Yes, I just - I could hear it going, and it bled down.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Oh, okay, I'll put it up some more then.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, LM connector fairings, two, orange, open.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, one of them is open; it got knocked open, I guess, during the dock - LM connector fairings, open.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: They're hard to reach; don't lose them.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Oh, boy, this is bad, because the next thing it says is the systems test meter at 4 Delta.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Can you hold a minute, Rusty?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, stand by, let me - I - I'll - Hold on there a minute, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Boy, those are tough to get.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I think I got it, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Don't lose them, because if you lose them, you can't get them back.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, you want to look at 4-D and --
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, if I can get down here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, connect and lock, and then look at it.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I'll never be able to hold my hand in there - until I get the --
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Apollo 9, this is Houston through Ascension.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We are mating the umbilicals right now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Understand you are connecting the umbilicals.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: I think so; let me check.
Boy, that's really tight in here.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, position the umbilic - Jim, Dave, what's the systems test meter read?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, yes, I know - I'll - I'll just --
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Position umbilical in slot and close fairing.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: You know where that slot is, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It's up behind there; you have to wind it around.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It doesn't fit in there too neatly.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Repeat for the second one - umbilical.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Boy, the lighting is atrocious on this one.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Man, that was soft as could be!
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Old Eagle Eye Fleegle sneaked it right in --
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We are going to have you for about another minute here at Ascension, and then we'll see you over Tananarive at about 44, and we would like to know the time of when you transfer to the CSM power, and I have a sunrise time any time you want it.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We transferred to CSM power at 3 hours 33 minutes and 0 seconds.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Houston, what oscillation reading on the system test meter through the LM power to about a half a volt to sometimes up to 3 volts.
It's in slow oscillation maybe every 10 seconds or so.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It's varying from a half to 3 volts slowly.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Pops open and pops back down sometimes to two.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: There is some smaller oscillations that occur at a period about every second.
It's been about 2 or 3 tenths of a volt.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Copy small oscillations 2 tenths to 3 tenths.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Hatch installation: align hatch in tunnel.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: You want to get up there and take a look at it before I (garbled), Dave?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: And what was the sunrise time, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: You want to look at it before --
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: If you got them in, they're in.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Just thought you might want to look and see what --
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: -- (garbled) like before you had - had to do it next time.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, let me - let me put these other umbilical covers back kind of out of the way.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, what the - Oh, my God!
Did that one scare the hell out of me!
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I just looked out the window and the LM wasn't there.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Let me get down here and look around one more time where I can see those bungees better.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: It's pretty hard to have a LM out there, isn't it?
Or not to have one with Jim in the tunnel.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, hatch installation: align hatch in tunnel step 1.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: First thing, I've got to rotate it here.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, it's on the checklist there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It's caught on this side, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I was just getting the checklist.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Say, Da - Dave, sunrise at 04 plus 08, I think they said.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I don't know; I found it floating over here in the middle of all that.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, pump handle, unstow; set to L and push to stop.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, these sunglasses weren't a very good idea, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: They scratched up the inside of my visors something fierce.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, we're ready to go to latch.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Looks like the outside to me.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Is the disconnect socket in L?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: You can - you should be able to see the latches over center, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I just was looking at the holes to see if it went in the right holes.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, that one there is okay; I can verify it.
Let me get my flashlight out.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, pump handle select is stow; and push to stow.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Pump handle select goes to stow.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, hatch pressure equalization valve, closed, clockwise.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, and the GET is now - huh - 03:39:15.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, install RHC number 1 on LEB mount.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Well, I'm going to put that back where we got it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, the cabin pressure is slowly building.
It's at 5 - I mean 4.6, now 7 [32.4 kPa].
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: This is a real place to stow something.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: What do you want me to do with my --
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Wait just a moment, there's a little -
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'm not sure if you're just bumping into me or whether you're trying to get me to - or whether you're trying to tell me something.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: It's a - it's a little of both.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Boy, I'll tell you, I've - I've knocked this doggone camera off more times.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, Rusty, I'm going to stow the hand controller back here.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Let me copy down a few other things here.
000, and now systems test meter is going from about 0.4 to 2, huh?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I don't know what it's going now; I can't see it.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, and I'm not sure whether you damage this wire more by putting it back in the holder or - or not.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Whether you damage this - this hand controller cable more by putting it in the - in the cable holder, or by leaving it alone.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Oh, man, I'm not going to do that anymore.
We need to put Rusty's suit hoses back up.
You can turn the tunnel lights out now.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: How's that tunnel look there, Colonel?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Not bad at all, as a matter of fact!
It's very easy to work in, Dave, except that it just takes a long time.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, the next thing I see is pre-LM ejection at 04:11 GET.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, now, Dave, could you look at the quantity on there?
I think the damn film is empty.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, do we really want to get this?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Well, it doesn't have another magazine for today.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Well, then let's not use it.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, I'll tell you, let's take a little.
You can take it when we get going.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, but don't you think we ought to take it coming out of SLA?
I think we ought to be able to --
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: -- have some film of coming out of the SLA.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, we'll do that, and - How about everybody yelling "Film" at me, huh?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I'll tell you what, when we come out, though, Rusty - You want to come out and pitch, though, right away.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'll just - I'll hold my finger right on the button.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, I - I don't think we're going to see much around the - We're not going to see the SLA out there really.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, Jim, you get to see -
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: From what I can see, Jim, I think we will.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think you will, and I think you need to do that.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: That's what we got it for.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You've already got the film in, huh, Dave?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, sir, and you can run a check, if you like.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: But we allocated it for other things, too.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, that RL on that COAS is neat.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I can see an RL distance --
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Houston, we've got the tunnel closed out and the hatch back in, and I guess we're getting set up now for the ejection.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, I'll tell you one thing I want to do is take off these sunglasses.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I heard you answer me, but it's unreadable at this time.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: The tunnel is closed out, the hatch is in, we are preparing for ejection.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: The hatch is closed out, and you are pressurizing.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, why don't we collect ourselves here?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I want to get strapped back in here,
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Jim, can I lower that one notch?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We're losing you here at Tananarive.
We'll see you over Carnarvon at about 59.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Oh, that's going to - I'm telling you that's going to really screw this flight up.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You want me to give you a hand, Dave?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We ought to make sure we get the Auto RCS select where it ought to be.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, I'd like to take my glove off, too, and get my helmet off, and get this damn - helmet - or - sunglasses off.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Boy, have I got some gouges on this visor!
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Not mine - I mean, this is just on the other visor.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, from screwing around up there?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That probe really is in the way (laughter).
You know it hangs down right in amongst everything.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Boy, did they ever scratch up that visor!
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Oh, you've got a - Oh, looks like you've got a bunch of scratches on the outside, Rusty.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: How could I get them on the outside?
I don't think I touched anything on the outside.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: That's what they look like to me.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And you're right; you really have massacred it!
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You've got to have a brighter COAS.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I couldn't even see it when we got down on the target.
I was going for that needle for the cross.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I could see it barely, barely.
On that white cross, you know?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: The background, the cross is all white.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We're ready to start the pre-ejection procedures.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, LM tunnel lights, on, right?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, back to the reading here.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, let me get back to the - the channel.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'm going to turn some lights down.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: You want me to load your DAP for you?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Rusty, did you check all the settings on the camera again, too?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And what mag have you got there?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Oh, hey, wait a minute, that's just right.
We're supposed to have 1 for LM ejection.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Look in there and see if 1 does anything else later on.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: See what else we do with 1.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, I'll give you a 3, 2, 1, mark, and S-IVB/LM - Here you go, Jim.
And you do that little switch there that doesn't have any stripes on it --
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And I'll go CMC - AUTO, and at 5 seconds I'll burn aft, pretty (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, we got the needle set.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, if one quad had to get screwed up, C was the best one.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Actually, I think we used more out of A than -
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: C is the one that was hurting -
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I wonder if that's a real - what the real quantity is there?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: It was this talkback - this talkback and this talkback were all barber pole.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Boy, that's a bad - bad scene.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I wonder if that means we're going to have to look out for those things every time we give it a jolt.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, as a matter of fact, Jim, we ought to check them right after we jumped off of this thing.
It might be something like that.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, if it looks like we're hung up for some reason, I'll go to the tightest deadband we've got and give her some aft thrust.
Trying to control pitch and yaw doesn't seem to be the thing to do.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Apollo 9, this is Houston through Carnarvon.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: According to the Langley study, you're better off not screwing around.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You know, your - your hand-controller action there didn't seem to have much effect when we were - before hard dock either.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Well, until that one final time, it seemed to - The - the translational controller did nothing.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Just did nothing, and I finally got the minimum impulse going in pitch, and that seemed to do as much as anything.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: But it doesn't do much there either.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: But Kotanchik was really gungy about that --
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We have got you now in good voice contact.
We will be giving you your go here shortly and take a look at you.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And Apollo 9, we would like to have you arm the logic buses.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And, Apollo 9, you are go for pyro arm.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Understood, and understand the ejection at 4 hours 11 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We - I gave you the sunrise time here as 04 plus 08.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: You went us to go on sunrise or at 04:11?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We would like to have you go at sunrise.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: That will put your evasive maneuver at 04 plus 11.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Listen, if you concur, we would sort of like to wait until we have good sunlight before we come off of that.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: And, Apollo 9, we're still showing your command module telemetry switch in accept.
We would like to have you go block on that.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: You are coming off of Carnarvon here, but we will be monitoring your ejection through an ARIA.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Those ARIAs make an awful lot of noise, Houston.
We have trouble hearing each other.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Why don't we ask them to - I'm going to turn my VHF (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: You're very loud and making all kinds of noise and unreadable.
We have to talk to each other here, so it would be best if you didn't transmit.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You're making very much noise on VHF; it would be better if you did not transmit to us.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Understand that you want the ARIA down.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: I think that would be better if the ARIA is out of it.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You ready to start pyro arm, two, going up.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: TVC servo power, two, AC 1/Main A.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, circuit breaker S-IVB/LM SEP, two, are closed (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, now we're standing by for the check (garbled) Okay, why don't we wait until (garbled)?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, you got the evasive maneuver --
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: All the thrusters look good, here.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Proceed to pitch down at 25 seconds.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I got the cameras still going.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: The camera is still running, yes.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I guess we got nothing to see right now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, we - we're pitching down, aren't we?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We ought to see the S-IVB coming into view.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Why don't you turn the camera off until we see it.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, at completion of the - You got the RHC and THC unlock?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I'm going to take the pictures.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: No, you can't see it through here, Rusty, yet.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Look at all those white stars!
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, now you can get one, Rusty.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: At the completion of the maneuver, hit an Enter.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You're going to get the Enter; I'm going to call 47 out.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, when we get to - Okay, coming up alright.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Looks like a clean SEP by the looks of the S-IVB.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Why don't you shut that off and take some --
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, you guys got the rest of this now?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, Jim, keep watching the S-IVB.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Because I can't see it very well anymore.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, why don't you call it, Dave?
I can't look out there and see in here, too.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, and at 3 minutes, you want to thrust aft for --
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Heck, we're going away from it, Dave.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Man, you can't even feel that, can you?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, we got 0.7 or 0.8 of a foot per second --
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, SECS pyro arm, two, off.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: It's just a warning light, Dave.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, I don't - How many pictures are they looking for?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We're in good shape on the controls; we're in minimum impulse, and we're stable.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Should be all set up, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Check - check it to make sure, though, because I might have just turned it then.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: It's going to be there a long while.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, I'll check it for you.
I can't see it; I can't tell if --
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Manual attitude, three, to minimum impulse.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: You know, we're not getting away from him very fast.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: EMS mode - EMS function to off.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: TVC servo power, two, to off.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Maintain S-IVB view through window hatch with RHC number 1.
I think that's about the end of the reading, here.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Could you see him real well, Jim?
Because I - He's right out in front of me.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, he's right in the middle.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Good, because he's right in the middle of my window, too.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I'm going to take the camera; I'm going to lead it here a little bit and point it, and why don't you take a few?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, it looks like he's gaining on us again.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You want me to go to 24 and take a few, Dave?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, I - I don't think 24 is going to show us any more than -
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: See if you can't get that moon over there in the background?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, that ought to be enough anyway.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, that ought to be enough.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We're just starting to lock on now, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, it goes to orbit rate at 04:25, so it's quite a ways out.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Except - Well, I tell you how we can tell.
If we had the old diastimeter out, we could figure it out; but I guess we don't.
Let me see if I can tell on the COAS.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We've got to get down underneath it, you know that?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: He comes back up again, though, Jim, according to those charts.
If the simulator is any good at all, Jim -
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Let me get a - a reading on him.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You're coming in very weak, but be advised we had a successful ejection and we are presently separating very slowly from the S-IVB.
We've got them in sight out of all of the windows.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Could you give me your ejection time, please?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We're not getting in gimbal lock, are we?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Well, it was right at 11, wasn't it?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: If you can read - the ejection time was 4 hours 8 minutes and 5 seconds.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Say the minutes again, please, Apollo 9.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: I wonder if the LM tunnel's leaking.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, he's a little more than 3 degrees.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, hell, we can't be closing.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: -- that vent was a lot more than I expected on the turnaround, Rusty.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We - I never did stop - I never took out what I put in on getting off of him.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, but even if he is venting, he - he still should be separating from us with that attitude.
He's the same dianeter as the thruster's throat right now.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Huntsville does not have valid two-way.
transcript.speaker: Unknown

tokens: If you read - we did copy your transmission of a successful ejection.
We did copy the time but --
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: He's not going down any farther.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: -- we would like for you to verify the minutes - if you can try it again.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Would you give me your ejection time again, please?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Thank you, and we'll see you over Hawaii at about 24.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Why don't you check, Rusty, and see what time this guy gets ready to do the maneuver?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: If you can read me, the S-IVB maneuver time is 25 plus 04.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We're talking to each other again.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: I'm measuring him against the thrusters.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: You know, he's still venting up this way at it, too.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: It's the only way he can vent.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Man, he's really venting a lot.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Boy, there are a bunch of pieces of plastic and stuff floating around out there, aren't there?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I just saw a piece of Scotch tape float by.
That was probably a piece of the LM.
The only thing I know with Scotch tape holding it together.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: He's sure not where he really ought to be.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We haven't done a thing yet, have we?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I don't know - Well, I'm keeping the attitude sort of squared away by pulsing, but he ought to be higher in the window - based on the --
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: They never had the vent model in the simulator, though.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, that's probably the difference right there.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Never had the vent model in the simulator.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'm going to put him over where you can see him, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: He's slowly closing the gap, though.

tokens: You think he's getting closer, Dave?

tokens: No, I mean - my visual gap, I'm sorry.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: He's - I don't think he's getting closer.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (garbled) sure isn't where he's supposed to be right now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Once he does his pitch to orb rate, he ought to go - which way - Let me see - He'll go down and start venting away from us.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, guys, you know what I think that O high flow is?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I'm not sure, but I have an idea that it's the fact that we got a little higher leak rate, right now --
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: -- and when the - and when the accumulators go, I think it might go overtime.
Because right now, we're reading - we're reading 0.6 on the leakage.
The cabin is up at - about - just about 5.0 [psi, 34.5 kPa].
We probably ought to fill up the first bottle.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Wait until we get through with lift-off.

tokens: Let me look at the flight plan and see where we stand here.

tokens: What was his maneuver time again?

tokens: You guys sing a nice song.

tokens: I'd say he vents about once a second.

tokens: I was - I was watching that before when Dave was trying to dock with him - It - He kind of blows and then it stops, and then it blows and then it stops.

tokens: It's the only way I can really (garbled)

tokens: What's the time of the - 04:25 is his time of maneuver?

tokens: Apollo 9, this is Houston through Hawaii.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Apollo 9, this is Houston through Hawaii.

tokens: We've been sitting here watching the S-IVB maneuver, and it's just about 90 degrees to our line of sight now.

tokens: The comm is beautiful now, Apollo 9; we had dropped our GOSS Conference was the delay.
And I would like to pass you the ignition time for the S-IVB,
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We might get a better one.

tokens: We are showing the S-IVB restart at 04 plus 45 plus 56.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: The S-IVB has completed its maneuver, and we would like to have a go from you to release the maneuver inhibit - the restart inhibit.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: The S-IVB has completed its maneuver, and we are standing by for its ignition.
We would like to have a go from you to release the restart inhibit.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We've just announced that we are quartering to the rear and above, and you have a go for restart inhibit.

tokens: You're fading, Stand by about a minute and we'll pick you up better.

tokens: We've got you now through the Redstone, and you were faded out on your last transmission there.

tokens: You have a go to release the restart inhibit.

tokens: You are a little weaker than Rusty, Jim.

tokens: I just was wondering; you weren't answering some of my transmissions.

tokens: Your last transmission was an ARIA at LOS coming off Hawaii there; we had about a 40-second break here.

tokens: But, I've got you real good now.

tokens: I missed what Jim said there.

tokens: Houston, we're going to be just about down his tailpipe.

tokens: It's better than being right off the nose, but let's see what somebody says here.

tokens: It's our understanding that the places not to be are directly above or below inside of 500 feet, so with that criteria, it sounds like you are doing okay.

tokens: And, Apollo 9, when your lead cuts in its afterburner you're expected to keep up.
